title: Israel: A Sign for the Nations
summary: “Now learn a parable of the fig tree; When his branch is yet tender, and putteth forth leaves, ye know that summer is nigh: So likewise ye, when ye shall see all these things, know that it is near, even at the doors.”(Mat 24:32-33). King Frederick the Great of Prussia (1712-1786) once asked his physician for proof of the existence of God. The physician is famously reported to have said, “Your Majesty, the continued existence of the Jews”. Against all odds, the Jewish nation has persevered despite persecution that at times reached genocidal proportions in various countries. Antisemitism is considered to be the world’s most successful hatred. It is unique in the annals of history because the Jews appear to be the only people whom sworn enemies in different eras had vowed to eliminate collectively as a people. Pharaoh, Haman, Hitler and Nasser of Egypt are some of the most prominent, but there were countless others with similar ambitions. Why has Satan so hated this people? The answer is simple, and provided by the Lord Jesus Christ himself. “Ye worship ye know not what: we know what we worship: for salvation is of the Jews” (John 4:22). The Law, the prophets, Psalms and the New Testament emanated from Israel. When the fullness of time came (Galatians 4:4), God sent forth his Son from Israel to save the nations. The 12 apostles were Jews and Jesus will return to Israel from where he will rule the nations with a rod of iron (Revelation 12:5). Let us follow the spiritual history of this nation and its message for us who look for the return of Christ.
